howItWorks.intro: This mouse sensitivity converter uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: What eDPI do pros use?
faq.answer: Most CS2 pros use 600-1200 eDPI (DPI × sensitivity). Valorant pros typically use 200-400 eDPI (equivalent after conversion). Lower sensitivity = more precision but requires more desk space.

faq.question: Is the Mouse Sensitivity Converter free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard practical calculation formulas trusted by users. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What mouse dpi should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate mouse dpi value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

faq.question: Can I use this calculator on my phone?
faq.answer: Yes, the Mouse Sensitivity Converter is fully responsive and works on any device — phone, tablet, or desktop. No app download needed.

sections.title: What Is the Mouse Sensitivity Converter?
sections.body: The Mouse Sensitivity Converter is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your mouse dpi, in-game sensitivity, target game, you get instant results including edpi, converted sensitivity, cm/360°. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting edpi right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your mouse dpi and need to find the right edpi. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Mouse DPI
howToSteps.description: Type or select your mouse dpi in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your In-Game Sensitivity
howToSteps.description: Type or select your in-game sensitivity in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Target Game
howToSteps.description: Type or select your target game in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your edpi, converted sensitivity, cm/360°. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your mouse dpi before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both edpi and converted sensitivity — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to get accurate results.
If you're unsure about your target game, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
